requirements.requested: alpaca_equity_ohlcv — daily OHLCV bars for EMA trend signal, realised volatility filter, entry/exit logic, and universe ADV screen
requirements.notes: Fully wired into the Lab pipeline as the default equity price feed. Covers 2016-present at daily and intraday granularity. All required computations (EMA, log-return vol, ADV) are derivable from this single feed with no additional sources.

prompt:
You are Talon's spec-drafting mode inside the Vires Research Lab.
Return only the structured object requested by the schema. Do not invent backtest results.

Your task has two parts:
1. Draft a StrategySpecV1 starting point for the operator to review.
2. Assess whether every data dependency is actually available in the catalog.

Data readiness rules:
- PASS only when every core requirement maps to AVAILABLE.
- WARN when requirements map to PARTIAL, or when a non-core nice-to-have is missing.
- BLOCKED when any core signal, entry rule, or exit rule requires missing data.
- When unsure whether data is core, treat it as core.
- Thin theses are allowed. Unavailable data is not allowed.

Idea sleeve: STOCKS
Idea title: Talon Smoke PASS2 20260430T070938Z
Idea thesis: Live smoke test for Talon drafting. Draft a stocks strategy whose only data dependency is Alpaca equity OHLCV bars. Use closing-price trend and volatility computed from OHLCV. The strategy must not require any sentiment, attention, fundamentals, options, macro, or alternative data source.

Data capability catalog (2026-04-30.1):
- alpaca_equity_ohlcv: Price OHLCV; AVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS; coverage=2016-present notes=Daily + intraday bars via Alpaca. Wired into the active strategy bank and the Lab pipeline. The default price feed for equities-sleeve research.
- alpaca_crypto_ohlcv: Price OHLCV; PARTIAL; sleeves=CRYPTO; coverage=2024-10-23-present notes=BTC and ETH only via Alpaca. ~1.5 years of history — sufficient for execution and recent-regime work, insufficient for multi-era analysis. Use ccxt_binance_crypto_ohlcv for longer history (research-only, no execution coupling).
- ccxt_binance_crypto_ohlcv: Price OHLCV; PARTIAL; sleeves=CRYPTO; coverage=2017-present (BTC); shorter for alts notes=Public Binance OHLCV pulled via ccxt for research backtests only. NOT wired into the executor — execution stays on Alpaca. Operator-pulled: no live connector, scripted backfills.
- alpaca_options_chain: Options chain; PARTIAL; sleeves=OPTIONS; coverage=live snapshot only notes=Live chain via Alpaca. NOT historical: no point-in-time reconstruction for backtests. Strategies that depend on historical chain state need a separate provider before they can be Lab-tested.
- iv_surface_constructed: Implied vol surface; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=OPTIONS; notes=Would require historical chain history we don't have. No connector and no construction pipeline. Strategies depending on IV surface skew/term structure should BLOCK.
- wikipedia_pageviews: Attention proxies; PARTIAL; sleeves=STOCKS, CRYPTO; coverage=2016-present (research backfills) notes=Wikimedia REST API is freely accessible; research backfill scripts exist. No production connector wired into the Lab pipeline yet — operator-driven pulls. Don't claim AVAILABLE until a daily-update connector lands.
- gdelt_full: Attention proxies; PARTIAL; sleeves=STOCKS, CRYPTO; coverage=2015-present (research backfills) notes=GDELT API is free; backfill scripts exist. No daily-update connector wired into the Lab. Same status caveat as wikipedia_pageviews.
- google_trends: Attention proxies; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS, CRYPTO; notes=No wired connector. Sample-rate noise + rate limiting on the public endpoint make it a v2-or-later candidate.
- apewisdom_top100: Sentiment; AVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS; coverage=2024-present (continuous) notes=Top-100 retail-attention rank with mention/upvote ratios. Wired as the canonical retail-sentiment feed for stocks. Aggregates WSB + adjacent communities; raw per-thread velocity is NOT separately exposed.
- reddit_wsb_velocity: Sentiment; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS; notes=Raw r/wallstreetbets per-thread velocity is NOT pulled separately. Use apewisdom_top100 for the aggregated signal. Strategies that genuinely require sub-Ape-Wisdom granularity should BLOCK.
- fundamentals_basic: Fundamentals; PARTIAL; sleeves=STOCKS; coverage=current snapshot only notes=Market cap + average daily volume reachable via Alpaca asset metadata. Not point-in-time. Strategies needing historical fundamentals (P/E, EPS surprise, balance-sheet items) are NOT covered.
- crypto_onchain: Crypto on-chain; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=CRYPTO; notes=No on-chain connector (Glassnode, Dune, etc.). Strategies referencing whale flows, exchange reserves, miner behavior, or staking metrics should BLOCK.
- macro_fred_subset: Macro; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS, CRYPTO, OPTIONS; notes=FRED series are operator-curated as needed for one-off research; no Lab-wired connector. Strategies depending on macro inputs (fed funds path, CPI surprises, yield curve shape) should BLOCK until a connector lands.

(2) Volatility filter: 20-day realized volatility (annualised standard deviation of daily log returns). Only trade stocks whose realised vol is between a lower bound (e.g. 15% annualised) and an upper bound (e.g. 80% annualised), filtering out both dormant and hyper-volatile names. The composite signal score = (EMA20 - EMA60) / EMA60, normalised cross-sectionally each day. raw_proposal.signal_logic: Compute two signals entirely from daily OHLCV bars sourced from alpaca_equity_ohlcv. (1) Trend signal: 20-day exponential moving average (EMA20) vs 60-day exponential moving average (EMA60). A stock is in an uptrend when EMA20 > EMA60 and in a downtrend when EMA20 < EMA60. (2) Volatility filter: 20-day realized volatility (annualised standard deviation of daily log returns). Only trade stocks whose realised vol is between a lower bound (e.g. 15% annualised) and an upper bound (e.g. 80% annualised), filtering out both dormant and hyper-volatile names. The composite signal score = (EMA20 - EMA60) / EMA60, normalised cross-sectionally each day. Long the top decile by score; flat or short the bottom decile (long-only variant: long top decile only).
raw_proposal.entry_rules: At the daily close, rank all universe members by the composite signal score. Enter long positions in the top-decile names that (a) have EMA20 > EMA60, (b) pass the realised-vol filter, and (c) are not already held. Positions are sized by inverse-volatility weighting within the selected basket. Execute at next-day open (or MOC if available) to avoid look-ahead on the signal close price.
raw_proposal.exit_rules: Exit a position when any of the following occur: (1) the stock falls out of the top two deciles on the next daily rebalance (rank-based exit); (2) EMA20 crosses below EMA60 (trend reversal); (3) realised vol breaches the upper bound (risk filter breach); (4) a hard stop-loss of 8% from entry price is hit intraday; (5) a portfolio-level drawdown circuit-breaker of 15% from the rolling 60-day equity peak triggers a full flat. Rebalance frequency: daily evaluation, with position changes executed at the following open.
raw_proposal.risk_model: Inverse-volatility position sizing: each position weight = (1 / sigma_i) / sum(1 / sigma_j) for all held positions j, where sigma_i is the 20-day realised vol of stock i. Maximum single-position weight capped at 10% of NAV. Maximum gross long exposure capped at 100% of NAV (long-only). Portfolio-level stop: flatten all positions if portfolio drawdown from 60-day peak exceeds 15%. No leverage. Cash held in remainder when fewer than 10 qualifying names exist.
raw_proposal.universe: S&P 500 constituents (or a liquid large/mid-cap proxy list of ~500 US equities) with price > $5 and 60-day average daily dollar volume > $10M, sourced from alpaca_equity_ohlcv. Universe is refreshed monthly using the most recent 60-day ADV computed from OHLCV data alone (no point-in-time fundamentals required).
required_data: alpaca_equity_ohlcv: daily OHLCV bars for signal computation (EMA, realised vol), entry/exit logic, and universe liquidity screening

acceptance_criteria.other: Annualised return > 8%; average holding period >= 3 days; strategy must show positive alpha vs SPY on a rolling 252-day basis for at least 55% of trading days in the backtest window.

raw_proposal.sweep_params: EMA short window: [10, 15, 20]; EMA long window: [40, 50, 60]; realised-vol lower bound: [10%, 15%, 20%]; realised-vol upper bound: [60%, 80%, 100%]; hard stop-loss: [5%, 8%, 12%]; top-decile cutoff: [10%, 15%, 20%]
raw_proposal.implementation_notes: All signals are derived exclusively from closing prices and volume in alpaca_equity_ohlcv. No external data sources are required. Universe construction uses 60-day ADV from the same OHLCV feed; no point-in-time fundamentals are needed because the liquidity screen is price/volume-based. Backtest window recommended: 2016-01-01 to present (full alpaca_equity_ohlcv coverage). Survivorship bias should be mitigated by using a fixed large-cap proxy list or a point-in-time index membership file if available; operator should note this limitation if using a static ticker list.

requirements.requested: alpaca_equity_ohlcv — daily OHLCV bars for EMA trend signal, realised volatility filter, entry/exit logic, and universe ADV screen
requirements.notes: Fully wired into the Lab pipeline as the default equity price feed. Covers 2016-present at daily and intraday granularity. All required computations (EMA, log-return vol, ADV) are derivable from this single feed with no additional sources.

raw_assessment.suggested_action: Proceed to backtest. All core data dependencies are satisfied by alpaca_equity_ohlcv alone. Recommend running the parameter sweep across EMA windows and vol bounds before committing to a single spec. Flag survivorship bias risk in the backtest setup if using a static ticker universe.
warnings: Universe construction relies on a static or operator-maintained large-cap ticker list. If the list is not point-in-time (i.e. it reflects current S&P 500 membership), the backtest will have mild survivorship bias. Operator should document this limitation in the research log.
The 8% hard stop and 15% portfolio circuit-breaker are heuristic defaults. They should be validated against the vol regime of the backtest period and may need tightening in high-vol regimes.

Daily rebalance with next-open execution assumes reasonable liquidity for the selected universe; slippage assumptions should be stress-tested, particularly for smaller names near the $10M ADV threshold.
